title: Porcelain board tile assemblies, systems, and methods for surface installations
abstract: The present disclosure provides embodiments of a porcelain board tile assembly. In an embodiment, assembly includes two or more porcelain board tiles cut from a reinforced porcelain panel product. A bottom surface of each porcelain board tiles to be adhesively affixed to a mounting wall when installed. Each reinforced porcelain panel product includes a porcelain slab and a structural core board. The structural core board includes a foam-core material having a surface area and a thickness greater than the porcelain slab. A top surface of the structural core board is positioned in contact with a bottom surface of the porcelain slab with an adhesive. A buffer region, defined by regions of the structural core board extending beyond outer peripheries of the porcelain slab, enhances protection from cracking when the reinforced porcelain panel product is handled. The structural core board is configured to attach to the mounting wall.
claims: 1. A porcelain board tile assembly, the assembly comprising: two or more porcelain board tiles cut from a reinforced porcelain panel product, each of the two or more porcelain board tiles having a top surface opposite a bottom surface and outer peripheries extending between and connected to the top surface and the bottom surface, the bottom surface to be affixed to a mounting wall when installed, the reinforced porcelain panel product defined by: (a) a porcelain slab having a top surface with a first surface area opposite a bottom surface, each of the one or more reinforced porcelain panel products having: an impact resistance at least 30% greater than an impact resistance of porcelain having a thickness of 6 mm or higher, and a breaking strength at least 40% greater than a breaking strength of porcelain having a thickness of at least 6 mm, and (b) a structural core board including a foam-core material and having a top surface and a bottom surface, the top surface of the structural core board having a second surface area greater than the first surface area of the porcelain slab and positioned in abutting contact with the bottom surface of the porcelain slab with adhesive therebetween and such that regions of the top surface of the structural core board extend beyond outer peripheries of the first surface area of the porcelain slab to define a buffer region, thereby to allow the buffer region to be visible when viewing the reinforced porcelain panel product from a front view of the top surface of the reinforced porcelain panel product and to enhance protection from cracking or chipping when the reinforced porcelain panel product is handled, the structural core board having: a fracture toughness greater than a fracture toughness of an associated porcelain slab, and a Young's modulus value lower than a Young's modulus value of the associated porcelain slab, the Young's modulus value of the structural core board ranging from about 2.0 GPa to about 14.6 GPa, at least two of the two or more porcelain board tiles also configured to be positioned in abutting contact, such that outer peripheries of a first porcelain board tile contact outer peripheries of a second porcelain board tile when installed.
